As plantas tรฉrmicas emitem carbono (CO2) como parte do processo de combustรฃo. Embora nรฃo sejam carbono zero, รฉ geralmente
reconhecido como neutro em carbono (CO2) quando a fonte รฉ biomassa plantada recentemente. Caso ocorra queima imperfeita,
podem ser gerados outros gases de efeito estufa.
Quando houver qualquer inconsistรชncia entre a versรฃo em inglรชs e a traduรงรฃo, prevalecerรก a versรฃo em inglรชs.
Auditor Notes
This statement is proof of the secure and unique redemption of the I-RECs stated above for the named beneficiary to be reported
against consumption in the country during the reporting year stated. I-RECs are assigned to a beneficiary at redemption and cannot be
further assigned to a third party. No other use of these I-RECs is valid under the I-REC Standard.
Where offset attributes are 'incl' the device registrant, who exclusively holds the environmental attribute rights, has undertaken never
to release carbon offsets in association with these MWh; 'excl' means carbon offsets relating to these MWh may be traded
independently at some point in the future.
Thermal plants emit carbon as part of the combustion process. While this is not zero carbon, it is generally recognised as carbon neutral
where the source is recent biomass.
Where there is any inconsistency between the English version and the translation, the English version shall prevail.

Informaรงรตes institucionais
Fundada em 1969 e pertencente ao reconhecido Grupo Minsur, empresa mineradora de
grande porte peruana, a Mineraรงรฃo Taboca atua na extraรงรฃo, beneficiamento e metalurgia
de estanho e minerais industriais, sendo uma das poucas empresas ativas no mercado
mundial a possuir mina prรณpria, localizada na Regiรฃo Amazรดnica, no municรญpio de
Presidente Figueiredo โ Vila do Pitinga, com longevidade estimada em um sรฉculo.
Alรฉm da sua planta do estado do Amazonas, a Mineraรงรฃo Taboca conta tambรฉm com uma
moderna estrutura em Pirapora do Bom Jesus, interior de Sรฃo Paulo, onde รฉ concentrado
o estanho, resultante da cassiterita extraรญda na Vila do Pitinga e que em segundo
momento รฉ fundido para obtenรงรฃo do estanho refinado, high grade, 99,9% de pureza. Na
planta de Sรฃo Paulo sรฃo tambรฉm criados e implantados processos de logรญstica,
sustentabilidade, acordos comerciais, entre outros que garantem o progresso da empresa
a nรญvel internacional.
Todo o potencial da empresa se dรก nรฃo sรณ pela riqueza natural das รกreas que ocupamos,
mas principalmente pelo compromisso dos nossos colaboradores, pelo bom trabalho
realizado e pelo respeito existente entre todos, que diariamente se dedicam na realizaรงรฃo
de atividades que nos consolidam no mercado mundial.
Sempre com foco no crescimento, a empresa investe em atraรงรฃo de jovens profissionais,
pesquisa mineral e tecnologias voltadas para a melhoria contรญnua de suas atividades,
sendo uma das principais empresas do mundo em seu setor.

Informaรงรตes institucionais
A Mineraรงรฃo Taboca foi adquirida em 2008 pelo grupo peruano Breca e atua de forma
sustentรกvel nos setores de mineraรงรฃo e metalurgia de estanho e minerais industriais, com
estabelecimentos nos estados do Amazonas e de Sรฃo Paulo. No estado do Amazonas, no
municรญpio de Presidente Figueiredo, estรก situada a unidade minero-metalรบrgica,
composta pela mina Pitinga โ a Mineraรงรฃo Taboca รฉ uma das poucas empresas ativas no
mercado mundial de estanho a possuir mina prรณpria -, pela planta metalรบrgica e por uma
usina hidrelรฉtrica, que garante ร  unidade autonomia na produรงรฃo de energia. Jรก no estado
de Sรฃo Paulo, no municรญpio de Pirapora do Bom Jesus, encontra-se a unidade metalรบrgica,
onde o concentrado de cassiterita (minรฉrio de estanho), proveniente de Pitinga, รฉ fundido
para obtenรงรฃo do estanho refinado (high grade). Registrado na Bolsa de Metais de
Londres (LME), o Estanho Mamorรฉ โ nome comercial do estanho refinado - รฉ altamente
competitivo e reconhecido no mercado, garantindo o teor mรญnimo de 99,95% de estanho
metรกlico. A Mineraรงรฃo Taboca atua, tambรฉm, nos mercados de Niรณbio e Tรขntalo, por meio
da liga metรกlica FeNb e FeTa, produtos obtidos da fundiรงรฃo do concentrado de columbita,
presentes na mina de Pitinga.

against consumption in the country during the reporting year stated. I-RECs are assigned to a beneficiary at redemption and cannot be
further assigned to a third party. No other use of these I-RECs is valid under the I-REC Standard.
Where offset attributes are 'incl' the device registrant, who exclusively holds the environmental attribute rights, has undertaken never
to release carbon offsets in association with these MWh; 'excl' means carbon offsets relating to these MWh may be traded
independently at some point in the future.
Thermal plants emit carbon as part of the combustion process. While this is not zero carbon, it is generally recognised as carbon neutral
where the source is recent biomass.

Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informaรงรตes sobre a performance da organizaรงรฃo, em comparaรงรฃo com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizaรงรตes do mesmo setor).
A MUSA, como forma de entender seu cenรกrio no que tange as emissรตes de gases de efeito estufa, realizou no ano
base de 2022, seu primeiro inventรกrio para apresentaรงรฃo ao GHG Protocol. Esse levantamento possibilitou a MUSA

outras empresas do ramo.

futuros irรฃo ajudar a traรงar uma curva que terรก por base uma avaliaรงรฃo mais concreta e criteriosa sobre o real
cenรกrio de emissรฃo das operaรงรตes. Apรณs esse amadurecimento, a MUSA pretende estabelecer metas para
descarbonizaรงรฃo de suas atividades, alinhada com as necessidades dos mercados interno e externo.

Informaรงรตes institucionais
Somos uma empresa que pertence ao fundo de investimentos britรขnico Appian Capital
Advisory, atualmente estamos em fase de operaรงรฃo da Mina Serrote, localizada em
Craรญbas, no Agreste Alagoano, na divisa com o municรญpio de Arapiraca. A Mineraรงรฃo Vale
Verde estรก presente na regiรฃo desde 2007.
A Mineraรงรฃo Vale Verde realizou a Implantaรงรฃo das obras do Projeto Serrote, empregando
cerca de 70% da mรฃo de obra local. Agora na Operaรงรฃo, essa mesma premissa se mantรฉm
com a utilizaรงรฃo de profissionais da regiรฃo.
O objetivo da MVV, de forma segura e responsรกvel, รฉ a operaรงรฃo de uma mina a cรฉu
aberto para o beneficiamento e produรงรฃo do concentrado de cobre e exportaรงรฃo do
minรฉrio majoritariamente para o mercado asiรกtico.
A expectativa รฉ de lavrar cerca de 4,1 milhรตes de toneladas de concentrado de cobre por
ano. A vida รบtil estimada da Mina Serrote รฉ de cerca 14 anos, mas pesquisas minerais em
andamento podem ampliar esse prazo no decorrer do empreendimento.
Com um novo jeito de fazer mineraรงรฃo, mais compacta e econรดmica, a planta de
beneficiamento da MVV abriga equipamentos dos processos de britagem, moagem,
flotaรงรฃo e filtragem.
Desde 2018, 100% do capital da empresa pertence ร  Appian Capital Advisory LLP โ em
nosso paรญs, com o escritรณrio do Grupo Appian Brazil. A companhia possui o compromisso
com as melhores prรกticas ESG, incluindo a conformidade com os Princรญpios do Equador e
os Padrรตes de Desempenho da IFC sobre Sustentabilidade Social.

Informaรงรตes institucionais
Lรญder em exportaรงรฃo de carne in natura na Amรฉrica Latina, maior entre todas as empresas
do Paraguai, principal exportadora da Colรดmbia e a mais diversificada companhia do
setor frigorรญfico sul-americano. Foi com esse posicionamento que a Minera S.A. (Minerva
Foods) completou, em 2017, 25 anos de atividades.
Com mais de 9,7 mil itens em seu portfรณlio, a Companhia atua na produรงรฃo e na
comercializaรงรฃo de carne in natura, couro e processamento de proteรญna bovina, suรญna e de

Argentina, no Uruguai e na Colรดmbia. Sรฃo duas plantas de processamento de proteรญnas -
no Brasil e na Argentina - e 24 unidades de abate e desossa.
Essa estrutura foi incrementada em 2017 com a conclusรฃo da aquisiรงรฃo, por meio das
subsidiรกrias do Paraguai, de 100% do controle da JBS Paraguay S.A., da Indรบstria
Paraguaya Frigorifica S.A., do Frigorifico Canelones S.A. e da JBS Argentina S.A., atรฉ entรฃo
de titularidade da JBS S.A. e da JBS Handels Gmbh. Adquiriu ainda nove unidades
frigorรญficas no Mercosul - cinco na Argentina, trรชs no Paraguai e uma no Uruguai -, alรฉm de
uma unidade de processados e um centro de distribuiรงรฃo na Argentina. Com essa
expansรฃo, elevou sua capacidade de abate em 52%.
De capital aberto, listada no segmento Novo Mercado da B3 S.A. - Brasil, Bolsa, Balcรฃo (B3
- antiga BM&FBOVESPA) e sediada no municรญpio de Barretos (SP), a Companhia exporta
para mais de cem paรญses, na รfrica, na รsia, nas Amรฉricas, nas Comunidade dos Estados
Independentes (CEI), no Oriente Mรฉdio, no Tratado Norte-Americano de Livre Comรฉrcio

localizados na Colรดmbia, no Chile, nos Estados Unidos, na Argรฉlia, no Irรฃ, no Lรญbano, na
Rรบssia, na China, no Egito, na Inglaterra, na Austrรกlia e na Nova Zelรขndia. Em 2017, criou a
Minerva Europe Ltd., subsidiรกria na Inglaterra para prestar serviรงos de consultoria e
assessoria na รกrea de comรฉrcio exterior.
Nos mercados internos em que atua, atende cerca de 57 mil clientes, aos quais vende e
distribui produtos prรณprios e de terceiros, com foco nos pequenos e mรฉdios varejistas.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informaรงรตes sobre a performance da organizaรงรฃo, em comparaรงรฃo com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizaรงรตes do mesmo setor).
A Companhia estรก sempre atenta aos riscos que podem ser provocados pelas mudanรงas climรกticas e tem essa
temรกtica incluรญda em suas principais discussรตes multistakeholder e desde 2014, elabora seu Inventรกrio de Gases do
Efeito Estufa, inicialmente contemplando somente suas unidades de abate.
Em 2015, passou a sistematizar a coleta de dados de seu inventรกrio, tornando o processo mais eficiente e
aumentando sua confiabilidade, uma vez que todos os dados inseridos no sistema passaram a ser evidรชncias e
verificados internamente. Desde 2017, as unidades controladas atuantes no processamento de carnes foram
incluรญdas no inventรกrio e em 2019, somou-se a ele as emissรตes provenientes de outros negรณcios da Companhia.
Os resultados dos inventรกrios tem sido utilizados como instrumento gerencial para avaliaรงรฃo de sistemas internos,
como a matriz energรฉtica e o tratamento de efluentes. Com base nessas avaliaรงรตes, investimentos sรฃo dedicados a
eficiรชncia energรฉtica e melhoria contรญnua de outros processos, apresentando resultados positivos.
Alรฉm do monitoramento das emissรตes de GEE atravรฉs de suas operaรงรตes, a Companhia realiza o monitoramento
geoespacial de sua cadeia de fornecimento de gado no Brasil e no Paraguai garantindo que 100% dela seja livre de
desmatamento. No Paraguai, a Companhia รฉ pioneira no desenvolvimento desse monitoramento e assim, contribui
com a diminuiรงรฃo das mudanรงas climรกticas.
As auditorias independentes de asseguraรงรฃo do processo de monitoramento da cadeia de fornecimento de gado da
Companhia realizadas em 2019, no Brasil e no Paraguai, atestaram mais uma vez que a Minerva Foods cumpre
100% com seus critรฉrios socioambientais.
4.2 Descriรงรฃo de indicadores de emissรฃo de GEE para as atividades da organizaรงรฃo. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
A Minerva S/A faz mensalmente a gestรฃo de suas emissรตes atravรฉs da coleta sistematizada de dados e monitora
suas emissรตes desagregadas por unidade operacional e controladas. Desde 2015, monitora a evoluรงรฃo do indicador
de emissรฃo por cabeรงa de gado abatida. Atravรฉs desse indicador รฉ avaliada a performance consolidada da
Companhia e tambรฉm, de cada unidade de negรณcio inventariada.
4.3 Descriรงรฃo de estratรฉgias e projetos para a gestรฃo de emissรตes de GEE.
Para gerenciar suas emissรตes diretas de GEE, a Companhia desenvolve o Inventรกrio de Gases do Efeito Estufa e vem
aprimorando as aรงรตes de melhoria desenvolvidas de acordo com o perfil de emissรฃo observado atravรฉs do
inventรกrio. Com base nesse e em outros resultados de estudos realizados internamente pela Companhia foi
possรญvel investir em projetos de eficiรชncia energรฉtica que em 2019, trouxeram uma reduรงรฃo de 5% no consumo,
compara ao ano anterior. Este resultado representa uma economia de 7.886.723 kWh.Para gerenciar suas emissรตes
diretas de GEE, a Companhia desenvolve o Inventรกrio de Gases do Efeito Estufa, com base na metodologia GHG
Protocol reconhecida e acreditada em todo o mundo, e vem aprimorando as aรงรตes de melhoria desenvolvidas de
acordo com o perfil de emissรฃo observado atravรฉs do inventรกrio. Com base nesse e em outros resultados de
estudos realizados internamente pela Companhia foi possรญvel investir em projetos de eficiรชncia energรฉtica que em
2019, trouxeram uma reduรงรฃo de 5% no consumo, compara ao ano anterior. Este resultado representa uma
economia de 7.886.723 kWh.

4.4 Informaรงรตes sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam clรกusulas vinculadas ร  elaboraรงรฃo de

4.1 Informaรงรตes sobre a performance da organizaรงรฃo, em comparaรงรฃo com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizaรงรตes do mesmo setor).
A Companhia estรก sempre atenta aos riscos que podem ser provocados pelas mudanรงas climรกticas e desde 2014,
elabora seu Inventรกrio de Gases do Efeito Estufa, inicialmente contemplando somente suas unidades de abate.
Em 2015, passou a sistematizar a coleta de dados para seu inventรกrio, tornando o processo mais eficiente e
aumentando sua confiabilidade, uma vez que todos os dados inseridos no sistema passaram a contar com
evidรชncias e a serem verificados internamente.
Desde 2017, as unidades de processamento foram incluรญdas no inventรกrio e em 2019, somou-se as emissรตes
provenientes de outros negรณcios da Companhia, tornando seu escopo completo.
2020 foi um ano de grandes avanรงos e a Companhia tornou-se a primeira empresa no setor a ter suas emissรตes
lรญquidas de escopo 2 zeradas atravรฉs da compra de Certificados de Energia Renovรกvel (I-RECs), garantindo uma
matriz energรฉtica global 100% limpa. No Brasil, foram adquiridos certificados de energia eรณlica e nos demais paรญses
da Amรฉrica do Sul, certificados de energia hidrelรฉtrica.
Ainda no campo de eficiรชncia energรฉtica, a Minerva Foods passou a contar com um parque de energia solar em sua
unidade de Bucaramanga na Colรดmbia. O parque solar conta com 1.471 painรฉis solares instalados na รกrea dos
currais, produzindo cerca de 1.964 kW/h de energia renovรกvel e reduzindo a emissรฃo de 204,75 toneladas de CO2
anualmente. A energia produzida por esse sistema equivale a 10% do consumo da unidade.
Outros avanรงos considerรกveis, foram o inรญcio da contabilizaรงรฃo e reporte das emissรตes de escopo 3 das categorias
de Transporte e Distribuiรงรฃo Upstream, Deslocamento de Funcionรกrios Casa-Trabalho e Viagens a negรณcios para as
operaรงรตes do Brasil e a realizaรงรฃo de auditoria de terceira parte de seu inventรกrio de GEE, trazendo maior
confiabilidade dos dados reportados aos seus stakeholders.
Os resultados dos inventรกrios tรชm sido utilizados como instrumento de avaliaรงรฃo de sistemas internos e
determinaรงรฃo de investimentos na melhoria contรญnua dos processos e, ao longo dos รบltimos anos, a Companhia
tem aprimorado sua gestรฃo e vencido o desafio do gerenciamento de dados em suas unidades em diferentes
paรญses, tema que foi detalhado no case divulgado pela Companhia em parceria com a WayCarbon. Acesse em:
https://d335luupugsy2.cloudfront.net/cms/files/5818/1619616406Case_Minerva_VF.pdf?utm_campaign=resposta_a
utomatica_da_landing_page_case_marfrig_-climas&utm_medium=email&utm_source=RD+Station.
Alรฉm do monitoramento das emissรตes de GEE de suas operaรงรตes, a Companhia รฉ pioneira na realizaรงรฃo do
monitoramento geoespacial de sua cadeia de fornecimento de gado em 100% do territรณrio do Brasil e em 75% do
territรณrio do Paraguai, garantindo que a originaรงรฃo do gado seja livre de desmatamento e mudanรงas do uso do
solo.
Como parte da busca contรญnua pelas melhores prรกticas na pecuรกria, a Minerva Foods tambรฉm deu inรญcio a um
projeto piloto, em parceria com a Embrapa, para avaliar o balanรงo de carbono na atividade.
O projeto reรบne mais de 20 produtores dos estados de Goiรกs, Mato Grosso, Rondรดnia e Sรฃo Paulo, no Brasil, onde
serรก aplicado o protocolo desenvolvido pela Embrapa. Com isso, a Companhia terรก mais informaรงรตes sobre
emissรตes geradas pela pecuรกria e oportunidades para aprimorar as prรกticas no campo, como armazenamento de
carbono no solo, recuperaรงรฃo de pastagens e aumento da produtividade, biodiversidade, entre outras. A iniciativa
tambรฉm deve ser aplicada nos demais paรญses, com apoio de organizaรงรตes locais.
Ademais, a Minerva tornou pรบblicos os seus Compromissos com a Sustentabilidade e sua estratรฉgia de combate as

desmatamento ilegal zero em toda a cadeia de abastecimento da Amรฉrica do Sul atรฉ 2030, ampliando o
monitoramento hoje existente no Brasil e no Paraguai, para os demais paรญses onde atua e desenvolvendo um

informaรงรตes confiรกveis aos seus stakeholders.
Os resultados do processo de auditoria foram bastante positivos e confirmaram o compromisso da Minerva Foods

e estocam carbono, protegem a biodiversidade e aumentam a resiliรชncia em cenรกrios adversos. Como direcionador

Esta declaraรงรฃo รฉ a prova da aposentadoria dos I-RECs declarados acima para que o beneficiรกrio nomeado possa declarar o consumo
no paรญs para o ano declarado neste relatรณrio. Os I-RECs sรฃo atribuรญdos a um beneficiรกrio na aposentadoria e nรฃo podem ser designados
a terceiros. Nenhum outro uso desses I-RECs รฉ vรกlido sob o Padrรฃo I-REC.
Quando os atributos de compensaรงรฃo sรฃo incluรญdos (incl), conforme acima, o Registrante do dispositivo, que detรฉm exclusivamente os
direitos de atributo ambiental, se comprometeu a nunca emitir compensaรงรตes de carbono em associaรงรฃo com esses MWh; excluรญdos
(excl), significa que as compensaรงรตes de carbono relativas a esses MWh podem ser negociadas de forma independente em algum
momento no futuro.
As plantas tรฉrmicas emitem carbono (CO2) como parte do processo de combustรฃo. Embora nรฃo sejam carbono zero, รฉ geralmente
reconhecido como neutro em carbono (CO2) quando a fonte รฉ biomassa plantada recentemente. Caso ocorra queima imperfeita,
podem ser gerados outros gases de efeito estufa.
Para obter informaรงรตes sobre o esquema de rotulagem, consulte o site do esquema. A listagem do esquema de rotulagem pode nรฃo
ser exaustiva.
Quando houver qualquer inconsistรชncia entre a versรฃo em inglรชs e a traduรงรฃo, prevalecerรก a versรฃo em inglรชs.
Auditor Notes
This statement is proof of the secure and unique redemption of the I-RECs stated above for the named beneficiary to be reported
against consumption in the country during the reporting year stated. I-RECs are assigned to a beneficiary at redemption and cannot be
further assigned to a third party. No other use of these I-RECs is valid under the I-REC Standard.
Where offset attributes are 'incl' the device registrant, who exclusively holds the environmental attribute rights, has undertaken never
to release carbon offsets in association with these MWh; 'excl' means carbon offsets relating to these MWh may be traded
independently at some point in the future.

No final de 2021 foi estruturada a รกrea de ESG/Sustentabilidade, que passou a gerenciar os inventรกrios de GEE. Foi

rodadas com os responsรกveis por inserir as informaรงรตes para auxilia-los no entendimento do processo.
Com relaรงรฃo a projetos voltados ร s emissรตes de 2021, as emissรตes diretas foram compensadas por meio de projeto
de recomposiรงรฃo florestal junto ร  Iniciativa Verde, programa Carbon Free. Alรฉm disso, foram adquiridos certificados
de energia renovรกvel (RECs) em volume equivalente ao consumo energรฉtico de 2021, que atestam a geraรงรฃo de
energia atravรฉs de uma fonte renovรกvel, com intuito de fomentar o mercado de energia renovรกvel com fator de

RINA Services S.p.A. (RINA), commissioned by Carbotrader Assessoria e Consultoria em Energia
Ltda., has performed the validation of the project activity โCachoeirao CDM Project (JUN1092)โ in
Brazil, on the basis of UNFCCC criteria for the CDM, as well as criteria given to provide for consistent
project operations, monitoring and reporting. UNFCCC criteria refer to Article 12 of the Kyoto Protocol,
the CDM modalities and procedures and the subsequent decisions by the CDM Executive Board.
In conclusion, it is RINAโs opinion that the project activity โCachoeirao CDM Project (JUN1092)โ in
Brazil, as described in the PDD version 4.0 of 02/01/2012, meets all relevant UNFCCC requirements
for the CDM and all relevant host Party criteria and correctly applies the baseline and monitoring
methodology ACM0002, โConsolidated baseline methodology for grid-connected electricity generation
from renewable sourcesโ, Version 12.2.0 of 25/11/2011.
The validation report was updated (Revision 1.2) to reflect the receipt of the letter of approval and that is
the only change that has been made compared to the version (Revision 1.1, dated 21/01/2011) listed in
the Brazilian DNA letter of approval, dated 28/03/2011. The only difference of the actual validation
report to the previous validation report revision 1.2 of 21/01/2011 is the updated version of the PDD
version 3.1 of 27/06/2011 and the correction made in the validation to address the โIncompleteโ received
from Executive Board on 24/06/2011.
The validation report was updated to Revision 1.4 to address the โIncompleteโ received on 30/12/2011
from the Executive Board. The version of methodology , related Tools and start date of crediting period
were revised

RINA
VALIDATION REPORT
1 INTRODUCTION
Carbotrader Assessoria e Consultoria em Energia Ltda. has commissioned RINA to carry out the
validation of the โCachoeirao CDM Project (JUN1092)โ project in Brazil.
This report summarizes the findings of the validation of the project, performed on the basis of UNFCCC
criteria for CDM, as well as criteria given to provide for consistent project operations, monitoring and
reporting.
1.1 Objective
The objective of the Validation is to have an independent evaluation of a project activity by a designated
operational entity against the requirements of the CDM as set out in decision 3/CMP.1, its annex and
relevant decisions of the COP/MOP, on the basis of the project design document. In particular, the
project's baseline, monitoring plan, and the projectโs compliance with relevant UNFCCC requirements
and host Party criteria are validated in order to confirm that the project design, as documented, is sound
and reasonable and meets the identified criteria. Validation is a requirement for all CDM projects and is
seen as necessary to provide assurance to stakeholders of the quality of the project and its intended
generation of certified emission reductions (CERs).
1.2 Scope
The validation scope is to review the PDD against the UNFCCC criteria for CDM.
UNFCCC criteria for CDM refer to Article 12 of the Kyoto Protocol, the CDM modalities and procedures
and the subsequent decisions by the CDM Executive Board.
Validation is not meant to provide any consultancy towards the project participants. However, stated
requests for clarifications and/or corrective actions may have provided input for improvement of the
project design.
2 METHODOLOGY
Validation was conducted using RINA procedures in line with the requirements specified in the CDM
M&P, the latest version of the CDM Validation and Verification Manual, and relevant decisions of the
COP/MOP and the CDM EB and applying standard auditing techniques.
The validation consisted of the following three phases:
* Document review;
* Follow-up actions;
* The resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final validation report.
The following sections outline each step in more detail.
2.1 Document Review
The PDD version 4.0 of 02/01/2012 /1/, in particular the applicability of the methodology, the baseline
determination, the additionality of the project activity, the starting date of the project, the monitoring plan,
the emission reduction calculations provided in the form of a spreadsheet, CERs JUN1092_v4โ, version 4
of 02/01/2012 /2/, the financial analysis spreadsheet โIRR_Cachoeirao_v3_1โ, version 3.1 of
27/06/2011 /3/ were assessed as part of the validation.
The following table lists the documentation that was reviewed during the validation.

2.3 Resolution of outstanding issues
The objective of this phase of the validation was to resolve any outstanding issues which needed to be
clarified for RINA's positive conclusion on the project design.
To guarantee transparency a validation protocol has been customized for the project. The protocol shows
in a transparent manner the requirements, means of validation and the results from validating the
identified criteria. The validation protocol consists of four tables; the different columns in these tables are
described in the figure below (see Figure 1). The completed validation protocol is enclosed in Appendix A
to this report.
A corrective action request (CAR) is raised if one of the following occurs:
- The project participants have made mistakes that will influence the ability of the project activity to
achieve real, measurable additional emission reductions;
- The CDM requirements have not been met;
- There is a risk that the emission reductions cannot be monitored or calculate.
A clarification request (CL) is raised if information is insufficient or not clear enough to determine
whether the applicable CDM requirements have been met.
A forward action request (FAR) is raised during validation to highlight issues related to project
implementation that require review during the first verification of the project activity. FARs shall not
relate to the CDM requirements for registration. CARs, CLs and FARs identified are included in the
validation protocol in Appendix A of this report.

3.3 Project Design
The โCachoeirao CDM Project (JUN1092)โ is located in Pocrane and Alvarenga cities, Minas Gerais
state, Brazil in the following Geographical Coordinates: 19ยบ 26โ 12ยดยด S and 41ยบ 36ยด 51ยดยด W. Coordinates
were cross checked against ANEEL Dispatch # 1,214, dated 23 April 2007 /9/, and found to be correct.
The project activity falls under category: โGrid-connected electricity generation from renewable sourcesโ
and Sectoral Scope 1- Energy industries (renewable/non-renewable sources).
The project is a renewable electricity generation project activity using hydro resource displacing grid
electricity that is partly generated based on fossil fuels, with electricity generated from renewable sources
and thus resulting in the reduction of emissions of greenhouse gases in the energy sector.
The Cachoeirรฃo SHP has an installed capacity of 28.05 MW and it is a project classified as Small Hydro
Power Plant according to the ANEEL Resolution # 652, dated 09/12/2003 /13/, that establishes that in
Brazil, to be classified as a SHP, the reservoir area must be less than 3 Km2 (300 ha) and the total installed
capacity must be between 1 MW to 30 MW. The installed capacity was confirmed during site visit
through the equipmentsโ plates. ACM0002. The authorized capacity is 27 MW and according to the
equipments plates the installed capacity is 28.05 MW. As per ANEEL # 407 it has to be revised if the
difference is greater than 5%. The project activity has a single reservoir area of 1.021 km2, confirmed
through the ANEEL Dispatch # 1,214, dated 23 April 2007 /9/.
At the time of the site visit, the project was implemented and operational. Rina verified the following
equipments during the site visit:
3 Generators: GE Motors (model 271R640 โ Nominal power 11,000 kVA, Power factor 0.85), serial
numbers 227001530, 227001531 and 227001532;
3 Turbines: Voith Siemens (Francis โ Nominal power 9,300 kW, Flow 22.45 m3/s), serial numbers
19,453, 19,454 and 19,455;
2 Meters: ION 8,600, serial number PT-0801A126-01 (principal/main) and ION 8,600 serial number PT-
0801A128-01 (backup/rearguard).
The project design engineering reflects current good practice; equipments described in the PDD were
confirmed durig the site visit through the equipmentsโ plates.
The starting date of the project activity is 09/03/2007, based on the Santa Maria Energรฉtica S.A. service
order to start the plant construction. EPC contract between Santa Maria Energรฉtica S.A. and Consรณrcio
Construtor Cachoeirรฃo mentions, in its item 48.1.5, that the EPC contract is valid after the emission of the
service order. Confirmed in the shareholders minutes of meeting of Hidrelรฉtrica Cachoeirรฃo S.A. (annual
general meeting, dated 24/04/2008, registered in the Minas Gerais State Board of Trade on 08/01/2009),
that the shareholders of Hidrelรฉtrica Cachoeirรฃo S.A. are Santa Maria Energรฉtica S.A. and CEMIG
Geraรงรฃo e Transmissรฃo S/A. In light of the provided evidences, the earliest date on which the project
participant has committed to expenditures related to the implementation or related to the construction of
the project activity is 09/03/2007, as per Glossary of CDM terms, version 5 /29/.

Being a renewable electricity project, the project activity will generate GHG emission reductions by

2
the opportunity for employment in rural areas and to better conditions of the local economy. A renewable
crediting period of 7 years has been chosen for the project, starting from 01/01/2012, or the date in which
occurs the UNFCCC registration, the one that occurs later.
The total GHG emission reductions from the โCachoeirao CDM Project (JUN1092)โ are estimated to be

2
reductions of 23,444 tCO e / year.
2
The project has an Assured Energy1 equal to 16.37 MW (average), (resulting in a Plant Load Factor of
58.4 % = 16.37 MW / 28.05 MW) that was confirmed in the ANEEL Decree # 18, dated 25 May 2007 /9/,
that defined the assured energy for the Cachoeirรฃo SHP. Therefore, the average energy generated per year
is forecasted to be 143,401 MWh/year (16.37 MW*365 days*24 hours). It is important to highlight that
the plant load factor is issued by ANEEL (Brazilian Electric Energy Agency), and the calculations were
established in the Resolution nยบ 169, of 3rd May 2001. Historical data is used in the calculus and the plant
load factor is specific for each power plant.
The Assured Energy of a hydroelectric plant is issued by ANEEL (Brazilian Electric Energy Agency), and
serves essentially two purposes:
(i) to establish an upper limit for energy supply contracts (PPAs), and
(ii) to define the share of each generating plant on the total amount of energy generated in the system by
hydro plants.
The Assured Energy of the Brazilian electric system is defined as the maximum energy production that
can be delivered almost continuously by hydroelectric plants throughout the years, simulating the
occurrence of each one of the thousands of possibilities of statistically created flow sequences, admitting
certain risk of not attendance to the load, that is, in determined percentile of the simulated years some
rationing is allowed up to a limit considered acceptable by the system. The determination of the Assured
Energy is associated to the conditions in the long term that each plant can supply to the system assuming
an specific risk criteria of non-attendance to the market (risk of deficit), considering mainly the
hydrologic variability to which the plant is submitted.
RINA was able to verify all the documented evidence listed above during the validation process and can
confirm that data and considerations are complete and accurate.
RINA confirms that the description of the proposed CDM project activity, as contained in the PDD
sufficiently covers all relevant elements, is accurate and complete and that it provides the reader with a
clear understanding of the nature of the proposed CDM project activity.

3.6.2 Identification of alternatives
According to the VVM v.1.2 para. 105 โThe PDD shall identify credible alternatives to the project activity
in order to determine the most realistic baseline scenario, unless the approved methodology that is
selected by the proposed CDM project activity prescribes the baseline scenario and no further analysis is
required.โ
Based on this information, the selected baseline scenario to the project activity as per the ACM0002 is
defined as โElectricity delivered to the grid by the project activity would have otherwise been generated
by the operation of grid-connected power plants and by the addition of new generation sources, as
reflected in the combined margin (CM) calculations described in the โTool to calculate the emission factor
for an electricity systemโ. Therefore, no additional analsysis is required for identification of alternatives.
The selected baseline scenario complies with the National requirements of ANEEL (Brazilian Electricity
Regulatory Agency), ONS (National Grid Operator) and FEAM (Minas Gerais environmental agency).
RINA can confirm that the alternative identified in the PDD is credible and complete.
3.6.3 Investment analysis
3.6.3.1 Choice of approach
The benchmark analysis was done in accordance with the Methodological tool "Demonstration and
assessment of additionalityโ (version 06.0.0) and Guidelines on the assessment of investiment analysis,
version 5. Among the three options available for investment analysis as discussed in the โTool for the
demonstration and assessment of additionalityโ, project participants have chosen the benchmark analysis
since the other two are not applicable. The simple cost analysis is not applicable because the project will
generate financial and economic benefits (from electricity sales) other than CDM related income. The
investment comparison analysis is not applicable either because the only alternative to the project activity
is the supply of electricity from a grid, which is not to be considered a similar investment project.
3.6.3.2 Benchmark selection
In Brazil there is not a widely accepted benchmark for SHPs projects nor does the Government require a
minimum profitability in projects of this kind. The project IRR (internal rate of return) was compared with
the yield on Government Bonds. Project participants have chosen a Brazilian Government Bond named
National Treasury Notes, Series C (NTN-C), with maturity for January 1st, 2031. It is placed on the
market by the Brazilian National Treasury by a Public Offering and its profitability is linked to Inflation
by the IGP-M Index. The brazilian government bonds presented by project participants as the project
benchmark is a popular and publicly investment option and it is defined by international rating agencies as
Investment Grade Bond. It is considered as low risk investment if it is compared to an investment in a
hydro power plant. Furthermore this bond profitability is linked to the IGP-M (General Index of Market
Prices) that is the main inflation index for industrial costs in Brazil. Therefore, the benchmark is in
accordance with the "Tool for the demonstration and assessment of additionality", paragraph 6, sub-step b
and VVM v.1.2 para.112.
In the PDD version 1, it was considered as the yield of paper the value quotation in one day for one year
(11/11/2009). Project participants added to the paper day quotation the average IGPM between 1999 and
2008. PP were addressed that this represents a misalignment of information, with sums of values that do
not represent the same period of time. Moreover, dates of NTC-N and IGP-M were after the starting date
of the project activity. In addition, taking into account that Brazil does not have a fully stabilized economy
and some inflation, an index like IGP-M (that is linked to the profitability of the NTN-C) had a non-linear
behavior in the last ten years, project participants were requested to consider a longer period for the
calculation of yield average, considering yearly averages and not quotation for specific days.

Revenues (Electricity Tariff *Generation)
The estimated net electricity generation supplied by the project plant to the grid was calculated based on
the assured energy (16.37 MW average) provided by ANEEL (ANEEL Decree # 18, dated 25 May 2007)
/9/.
The plant load factor was provided by the ANEEL (Brazilian Electric Energy Agency) , therefore it is in
line with the requirements of the โGuidelines for the reporting and validation of plant load factorsโ /33/.
As already commented (report item 3.3), the Assured Energy of an hydroelectric plant is issued for each
plant by ANEEL (Brazilian Electric Energy Agency), and serves essentially two purposes: (i) to establish
an upper limit for energy supply contracts (PPAs), and (ii) to define the share of each generating plant on
the total amount of energy generated in the system by hydro plants.
The Assured Energy of the Brazilian electric system is defined as the maximum energy production that
can be delivered almost continuously by hydroelectric plants throughout the years, simulating the
occurrence of each one of the thousands of possibilities of statistically created flow sequences, admitting
certain risk of not attendance to the load, that is, in determined percentile of the simulated years some
rationing is allowed up to a limit considered acceptable by the system. The determination of the Assured
Energy is associated to the conditions in the long term that each plant can supply to the system assuming
an specific risk criteria of non-attendance to the market (risk of deficit), considering mainly the
hydrologic variability to which the plant is submitted (information taken from ANEEL /40/ . It important
to highlight that the calculations for the assured energy was established by the ANEEL Resolution nยบ 169,

investment costs were compared to the average investment costs to construct SHPs in Brazil, and the
project activity has a proper and conservative correspondence with the R$ 5 million/ installed kW to R$
5.5 million/ installed kW found in the literature /46/.

3.6.5 Common practice analysis
The PDD version 4 of 02/01/2012 โCachoeirao CDM Project (JUN1092)โ assessed the Common Practice
Analysis, based on the Methodological tool "Demonstration and assessment of additionalityโ, version
06.0.0โ /7/ and on the โGuidelines on Common Practiceโ/52/.
The Methodological tool "Demonstration and assessment of additionalityโ defines on its step 4a (1):
โProjects are considered similar if they are in the same country/region and/or rely on a broadly similar
technology, are of a similar scale, and take place in a comparable environment with respect to regulatory
framework, investment climate, access to technology, access to financing, etc. Other CDM project
activities (registered project activities and project activities which have been published on the UNFCCC
website for global stakeholder consultation as part of the validation process) are not to be included in this
analysisโ.
Based in above assumptions (Tool/Guideline), the following steps were considered:
Step 1: Calculate applicable output range as +/-50% of the design output or capacity of the proposed
project activity โ project participants analyzed plants with installed capacity from 14.02MW and
42.08MW (+/- 50% of the SHP Cachoeirรฃo).
Step 2: In the applicable geographical area, identify all plants that deliver the same output or capacity,
within the applicable output range calculated in Step 1, as the proposed project activity and have
started commercial operation before the start date of the project. Registered CDM project activities
shall not be included in this step - project participants considered in PDD version 4 power plants in
Brazil within the same output or capacity as calculated in the step 1 above and have started operation
before start date of the crediting period 09/03/2007. The total of 11 power plants within the defined range

RINA can confirm that all data, rationales, assumptions, justifications and documentation provided by the

By assessing the evidences presented and cross-checking the information, RINA considers that the
reasoning for the proposed project additionality demonstration is credible and reasonable, i.e. the
proposed project activity has the ability to reduce anthropogenic emissions of greenhouse gases by
sources below those that would have occurred in the absence of the proposed CDM project activity.

measurement of achieved emission reductions.
RINA has checked all the parameters presented in the monitoring plan against the requirements of the
methodology and no deviations relevant to the project activity have been found.
RINA confirms that the monitoring arrangements described in the monitoring plan are feasible within the
project design, and the means of implementation of the monitoring plan are sufficient to ensure that the
emission reductions achieved by/resulting from the proposed CDM project activity can be reported ex

3.7.3 Management system and quality assurance
The energy delivered to the grid will be measured and recorded continuously (hourly reading and
recorded monthly) through electricity meters that complies with national standards. The National Grid
Operator (ONS) and Electric Power Commercialization Chamber (CCEE) are responsible for the
definition of the technical requirements of energy measurements for billing. The QA/QC procedures for
the monitoring of the energy delivered to the grid described in the PDD are in line with the applied
methodology. The electricity supplied to the grid will be monitored by electronic calibrated and inviolable
(sealed) energy meters. The data from the energy meters will be cross checked with the invoices of energy
sales or with the CCEE databank.
Metersโ calibration procedures (frequency) will follow the ONS โGrid Proceduresโ: Module 12, Sub-
module12.3. The project owners shall always follow the rules of the relevant bodies (e.g. ONS and

5 VALIDATION OPINION
RINA Services Spa (RINA) has performed validation of the project activity โCachoeirao CDM Project
(JUN1092)โ in Brazil, with regard to the relevant requirements for CDM activities.
The review of the project design document and the subsequent follow-up interviews have provided RINA
with sufficient evidence to determine the fulfillment of the stated criteria.
The host Party is Brazil. The Party fulfills the participation criteria and has approved the project and
authorized the project participants Carbotrader Assessoria e Consultoria em Energia Ltda. and
Hidrelรฉtrica Cachoeirรฃo S.A.. The DNA from Brazil confirmed that the project assists in achieving
sustainable development. No Annex I party has yet been identified.
The project correctly applies the approved baseline and monitoring methodology ACM0002,
โConsolidated baseline methodology for grid-connected electricity generation from renewable sourcesโ,
Version 12.2.0 of 25/11/2011.
By generating renewable energy from hydropower plant the project results in reduction of CO emissions
2
that are real, measurable and give long-term benefits to the mitigation of climate change. It is
demonstrated that the project is not a likely baseline scenario. Emission reductions attributable to the
project are hence additional to any that would occur in the absence of the project activity.
The total GHG emission reductions from the โCachoeirao CDM Project (JUN1092)โ are estimated to be
164,108 tCO e during the first renewable 7 years renewable crediting period, resulting in annual average
2
emission reductions of 23,444 tCO e / year. . The emission reduction forecast has been checked and it is
2
deemed likely that the stated amount is achivied given that the underlying assumptions do not change.
The monitoring plan provides for the monitoring of the projectโs emission reductions. The monitoring
arrangements described in the monitoring plan are feasible within the project design and it is RINAโs
opinion that the project participants are able to implement the monitoring plan.
In conclusion, it is RINAโs opinion that the project activity โCachoeirao CDM Project (JUN1092)โ in
Brazil, as described in the PDD version 4.0 of 02/01/2012, meets all relevant UNFCCC requirements for
the CDM and all relevant host Party criteria and correctly applies the baseline and monitoring
methodology ACM0002, โConsolidated baseline methodology for grid-connected electricity generation
from renewable sourcesโ, Version 12.2.0 of 25/11/2011 /5/.
RINA thus requests registration of the project as a CDM project activity.

CDM VALIDATION PROTOCOL
This document contains a generic Validation Protocol for CDM projects, which must be seen in conjunction with the CDM Validation and Verification Manual and the Validation
Report Template. The entries in the protocol should be adjusted and amended as appropriate to prepare for the validation of a particular project.
This validation protocol serves the following purposes:
๏ท It organizes, details and clarifies the requirements a CDM project is expected to meet; and
๏ท It ensures a transparent validation process by inducing the Validator to document how a particular requirement has been validated and which conclusions have been reached;
This protocol contains two tables with generic requirements for validation projects. Table 1 shows the requirements that the GHG emission reduction project will be validated against.
Table 2 consists of a checklist with validation questions related to one or more of the requirements in Table 1. The checklist questions may not be applicable for all investors, and
should not be viewed as mandatory for all projects. Where a finding is issued, a corrective action request or clarification request are stated. The resolution and final conclusions of
these requests should be described in Table 3 of this protocol.
Before this generic validation protocol can be applied to validate a specific project, the Validator must review and adjust/amend the protocol to make it applicable to individual project
characteristics and circumstances as well as individual investor criteria. The application of the Validatorโs professional judgment and technical expertise should ensure that checklist
amendments cover all necessary specific project requirements that have impact on project performance and acceptance of the project. Given the above, the checklist part of the
protocol is neither exhaustive nor prescriptive.

Determine the appropriate analysis method
Among the three options available for investment
analysis as discussed in the โTool for the
demonstration and assessment of additionalityโ,
project participants have chosen the benchmark
analysis since the other two are not applicable. The
simple cost analysis is not applicable because the
project will generate financial and economic
benefits (from electricity sales) other than CDM
related income. The investment comparison
analysis is not applicable either because the only
alternative to the project activity is the supply of
electricity from a grid, which is not to be
considered a similar investment project.
Apply benchmark analysis
In Brazil there is not a widely accepted benchmark
for SHP projects nor does the Government require a
minimum profitability in projects of this kind. The

Summary:
ICONTEC has performed the validation of the project: SHP Itaguaรงu CDM Project (JUN 1146), Brazil on the basis of
UNFCCC criteria for the CDM, as well as criteria given to provide for consistent project operations, monitoring and
reporting. UNFCCC criteria refer to Article 12 of the Kyoto Protocol, the CDM modalities and procedures and the
subsequent decisions by the CDM Executive Board. This validation report summarizes the findings of the validation.
The proposed project activity under validation process is based on methodology AMS-I.D.-Grid connected renewable
electricity generation โVersion 17. The project consists on the development of a new small hydroelectric power plant that
takes advantage of the waters of the Pitanga River, in the Paranรก State, Brazil. The water flow will allow an installed
capacity of 14.22 MW and annual supply of electricity to the grid of 74,578.5 MWh. Electricity will be transmitted through a
transmission line of 34.5 kV which joins the SHP Itaguaรงu plant with the Brazilian SIN by the Pitanga substation.
The validation process consisted of the following three phases: i) a desk review of the project design documents, ii) follow
up interviews with project stakeholders and iii) the resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final validation
report and opinion. The first output of the validation process was the list of requests presented in Table 2 of Appendix A.
In summary, it is ICONTECโs opinion that the project SHP Itaguaรงu CDM Project (JUN 1146), Brazil, as described in the
version 2 of the project design document meets all relevant UNFCCC requirements for the CDM and all relevant host
country criteria and correctly applies the baseline and monitoring methodology AMS-I.D. Hence, ICONTEC requests the
registration of the project as CDM project activity.

1. INTRODUCTION
Itaguaรงu Energia S/A and Carbotrader Assessoria e Consultoria em Energia Eireli have
commissioned ICONTEC to perform the Validation of SHP Itaguaรงu CDM Project
(JUN1146), Brazil (hereafter called โthe projectโ).
This report summarizes the findings of the validation of the project, which was performed
on the basis of UNFCCC criteria, as well as criteria given to provide for consistent project
operations, monitoring and reporting.
According to the documentation of the project activity, the project consists on the
development of a new small hydroelectric power plant that takes advantage of the waters
of the Pitanga River, in the Paranรก State, Brazil. The water flow will allow an installed
capacity of 14.22 MW and annual supply of electricity to the grid of 74,578.5 MWh.
Electricity will be transmitted through a transmission line of 34.5 kV which joins the SHP
Itaguaรงu plant with the Brazilian SIN by the Pitanga substation.
1.1 OBJECTIVE
The purpose of a validation is to have the opinion of an independent third party in order to
assess the projectยดs design; in particular, the project's baseline, monitoring plan, and the
projectโs compliance with relevant UNFCCC criteria. Host Partyโs criteria are validated in
order to confirm that the project design, as documented, is sound reasonable and meets
the identified criteria. Validation is a requirement for all CDM projects and is seen as
necessary to provide assurance to stakeholders of the quality of the project and its
intended generation of certified emission reductions (CERs).
1.2 SCOPE
The scope of the validation process involves an independent and objective revision to
determine that the project design meets the following criteria:
๏ง UNFCCC criteria: The Kyoto Protocol Article 12 criteria, modalities and procedures
for CDM (Marrakech Accords) and the relevant decisions by the CDM Executive
Board, and
๏ง Host Party criteria: National CDM requirements, including sustainable development
priorities, and potential specific requirements contained in, for example, the
preliminary approval by Designated National Authority or project agreements
between involved parties.
ICONTEC, based on its ethics code and internal procedures for carrying out validation,
verification and certification audits of CDM project activities (which, in turn, are based on
the validation and verification standard) focused on the identification of significant risks for
CER generation, and verification of the mitigation.
The validation does not signify the providing of any consulting for the project participants.
However, stated requests for clarifications and/or corrective actions may have provided
input for improvement of the project design.

1.3 GHG PROJECT DESCRIPTION

The Project consists of a new hydroelectric power plant to take advantage of the water of
the Pitanga River with a design flow of 12.18 m3/s, a reservoir area of 0.340 Km2, and a
installed power of 14.22 MW /10/. According to the Brazilian Agency ANEEL/12/, the
project is considered a small hydroelectric Power Plant, because the reservoir is less than
3 Km2 (โrun of riverโ) and the generation capacity is in the range of 1 to 30 MW.
ICONTEC confirmed through the document review process that the install capacity quoted
in PDD (14.22MW) will be the installed capacity of the generator for renewable electricity
generating units that involve turbine-generator systems, in accordance with PS, paragraph
82 (a).
IN-P-CC-01-F-011 Page 7 of 56 Approved: 2012/07/30
Version 00
VALIDATION REPORT VVS
The Project diverts flow from the Pitanga River and conveys the diverted flow through a
conveyance canal to a forebay and then through a penstock to a powerhouse equipped
with two identical Francis turbine units. The powerhouse tailrace discharges back to the
Pitanga River. The net electricity supplied by the project activity to the Brazilian grid will be
measured by a commercial measurement system installed in the Pitanga substation
located at the interconnection point to the national grid (which belongs to Copel /17/). The
electricity will be transmitted through a transmission line of 34.5 kV with a length of 34.7
kms. ICONTEC asked PP to include a better description of the electric connection inside
PDD (See CL3, Table A.2, Annex A). The finding was closed based on the PDD version 2.
The description of the technology to be applied provides transparent information to
evaluate its impact on the greenhouse gas balance. The credibility of this information has
been validated by reviewing the technical documentation of the proposed project activity
including, inter alia: project description sent to ANEEL /13.11/, line diagram from Copel /5/,
the authorizations emitted by ANEEL /6/, the environmental licenses /9/ and the EIA /8/.
During onsite visit, ICONTEC verified that the project activity construction is at 60%
completion, in accordance with the project schedule presented by PP /7/. ICONTEC also
confirmed that the start of SHP construction was the first May 2011. The PP estimates the
January 31st 2013 as the date of start of the commercial operation.
The baseline scenario is the electricity delivered to the grid by the project activity that
otherwise would have been generated by the operation of grid-connected power plants or
by the addition of new generation sources. The baseline of the proposed project activity is
further validated in section 3.4 of this report. With respect to the contribution of the project
in the greenhouse gases emissions mitigation (GHG), the project activity reduces the
emissions of these gases avoiding the entrance in operation of thermoelectric units (to the
Brazilian SIN) that burn fossil fuel in its operation.
During the construction of the hydroelectric power plant, around 100 new jobs have been
generated. For the operation phase, the PP considers that around 6 permanent jobs will
be required (plus the external maintenance contracts).
The GHG project considered can be classified as a CDM project in Sectoral Scope 1,
Energy industries (renewable/non-renewable sources), according to the List of Sectoral
Scopes of UNFCC. The project activity is Type (i) โrenewable energy project activities with
a maximum output capacity equivalent to up to 15 megawattsโ, as indicated by the
manufacturer of the equipment/10/. ICONTEC confirmed that the proposed small-scale
project activity is not a debundled component of a large-scale project activity, based on the
Guidelines on assessment of de-bundling for SSC project activities (version 3)/18/.
ICONTEC verified that the project start date is December 12th/ 2010, when the turbines
and hydro mechanicals equipments contracts were signed /10/. The expected operational
lifetime of project activity is 30 years, according to the technical documentation reviewed
by ICONTEC during on site visit /10/. ICONTEC also confirmed, through these contracts,
that the technology and equipment to be installed for the project activity, will be
manufactured in Brazil.
IN-P-CC-01-F-011 Page 8 of 56 Approved: 2012/07/30
Version 00
VALIDATION REPORT VVS
The starting date of the crediting period is expected on February 1st/ 2013, based on the
project schedule /7/. A CAR (CAR 5, Annex A, Table A.2) was raised in order to clarify the
date based on the documentation found out by ICONTEC during the on site visit. The

2. METHODOLOGY
The validation consists of the following three phases:
i) A desk review of the project design documents
ii) Follow up interviews with project stakeholders
iii) The resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final validation report and
opinion.
As mentioned in clause 1.2 of this report ICONTEC, based on its ethics code and internal
procedures, carries out validation, verification and certification audits of CDM project
activities (which, in turn, are based on the validation and verification manual) focused on
the identification of significant risks for CER generation, as well as the verification of the
contribution to climate change mitigation.
These internal procedures define the validation protocol which consists of three tables.
The different columns in these tables are described in Figure 1 โValidation protocol
tablesโ.
The validation protocol resulting from the Validation of SHP Itaguaรงu CDM Project
(JUN1146), Brazil is enclosed in Annex A (Table A.1) of this report.
Findings established during the validation can be seen as:
๏ท A non-fulfillment of validation protocol criteria, or
๏ท An identified risk to the fulfillment of the project objectives
The findings could take the form of a Corrective Action Request (CAR), Forward action
request (FAR) or a Clarifications Request (CL).
Corrective action requests (CAR) are issued, where:
i) The project participants have made mistakes which directly influence the ability
of the project activity to achieve real, measurable and additional emission
reductions;
ii) The CDM requirements have not been met; or
iii) There is a risk that emission reductions cannot be monitored or calculated
A Forward Action Request (FAR) is made to highlight issues related to project
implementation that will require review during the next verification of the project activity.
A Clarification (CL) is required where information is insufficient, or not clear enough to
establish whether a requirement is met.

3.3 PROJECT DESIGN
The project activity has been developed using the methodology AMS-I.D, version 17, โGrid
connected renewable electricity generationโ.
According to this methodology (point 9), the spatial extent of the project boundary includes
the project power plant and all power plants connected physically to the electricity system
to which the project activity power plant is connected to, that is the Brazilian SIN. The
project boundary has been basically determined by means of: documental review of line
diagrams which explain the connection of the project activity to Brazilian SIN /5/ and the
technical information presented to ANEEL in order to obtain the connection authorizations
/13.11,6/, which shows the connection to the SIN in the Copel Pitanga Substation; on-site
visit (including the substation and the transmission line) and the interviews (section 2.1).
Consequently, ICONTEC is able to confirm the identified boundary (section B.3, PDD).
ICONTEC also confirms the selected sources and gases are justified for the project
activity.
With respect to the contribution of the project in the greenhouse gases emissions
mitigation (GHG), the project activity reduces the emissions of these gases avoiding the
entrance in operation of thermoelectric units (to the Brazilian SIN) that burn fossil fuel in its
operation,
The project complies with the applicability criteria of the methodology as was verified by ICONTEC,
as follows. The assessment was carried out for each applicability criteria and included among
others the compliance check of the local project setting with the applicability conditions in regard to
baseline setting and eligible project measures. This assessment also included the review of
secondary sources (especially interviews and main equipments and civil works contracts /10/),
which sustain that applicability conditions are being followed.

3.4 BASELINE DETERMINATION
The baseline scenario is described as the electricity delivered by the project activity to the
Brazilian interconnected grid that would have otherwise been generated by the operation
of the grid-connected power plants and by the addition of new generation sources.
As stated in the approved consolidated baseline methodology AMS-I.D., the baseline
emissions are the product of the grid emission factor (EF ) times the electricity

On these bases, Equity IRR obtained was 7.65%, which, compared with the IRR

without the CERs revenues. Including CERs revenues, the Equity IRR becomes 8.01%,
remaining lower than the IRR benchmark.
Sensitivity analysis
A sensitivity analysis was performed by means of which the parameters Total Investment,
Assured Energy, Energy Price and O&M costs were tested in order to check the financial
impact of variations in these parameters, by determining the necessary variations to get
the IRR Benchmark (Breakeven point).

5. VALIDATION OPINION
ICONTEC has performed a validation of the project โSHP Itaguaรงu CDM Project
(JUN1146), Brazilโ. The validation was performed on the basis of UNFCCC criteria for the
Clean Development Mechanism and host country criteria, as well as criteria given to
provide for consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting.
The review of the Project Design Documentation and the subsequent follow up interviews
provided ICONTEC with sufficient evidence to determine the fulfillment of the stated
criteria.
The project activity is being proposed as unilateral project by Itaguaรงu Energia S/A and
Carbotrader Assessoria e Consultoria em Energia Eireli. Brazil has provided approval of
voluntary participation and meets all requirements to participate in CDM. The Brazilian
DNA confirmed that the project helps in achieving sustainable development.
The project correctly applies the methodology AMS-I.D.-Grid connected renewable
electricity generation โVersion 17.
The project consists on the development of a new small hydroelectric power plant that
takes advantage of the waters of the Pitanga River, in the Paranรก State, Brazil. The water
flow will allow an installed capacity of 14.22 MW and annual supply of electricity to the grid
of 74,578.5 MWh. Electricity will be transmitted through a transmission line of 34,5 kV
which joins the SHP Itaguaรงu plant with the Brazilian SIN by the Pitanga substation. It is
demonstrated that the project is not a likely baseline scenario. Emission reductions
attributable to the project are hence additional to any that would occur in the absence of
the project activity.
The total emission reductions from the project are estimated to be on the average of
14,818 tCO e per year over the selected 7 year crediting period. The emission reduction
2
forecast was checked and it is deemed likely that the stated amount is achieved because
the underlying assumptions do not change.
IN-P-CC-01-F-011 Page 28 of 56 Approved: 2012/07/30
Version 00
VALIDATION REPORT VVS
In summary, it is ICONTECโs opinion that the โโSHP Itaguaรงu CDM Project (JUN1146),
Brazilโ as described in the PDD version 2 (20/08/2012), meets all relevant UNFCCC
requirements for the CDM and all relevant host country criteria and correctly applies the
baseline and monitoring methodology AMS-I.D version 17. ICONTEC thus requests the
registration of the project as a CDM project activity.

Auditor Notes
This statement is proof of the secure and unique redemption of the I-RECs stated above for the named beneficiary to be reported
against consumption in the country during the reporting year stated. I-RECs are assigned to a beneficiary at redemption and cannot be
further assigned to a third party. No other use of these I-RECs is valid under the I-REC Standard.
Where offset attributes are 'incl' the device registrant, who exclusively holds the environmental attribute rights, has undertaken never
to release carbon offsets in association with these MWh; 'excl' means carbon offsets relating to these MWh may be traded
independently at some point in the future.
Thermal plants emit carbon as part of the combustion process. While this is not zero carbon, it is generally recognised as carbon neutral
where the source is recent biomass.
